objective: Introduce the exact officeparser 7.4.0 runtime dependency as an isolated prerequisite for the F-0082 DOCX, PPTX, and extractable-PDF conversion partition, without importing it into product code, changing runtime behavior, or claiming any F-0082 product closure.
blockedFiles: any product implementation, converter invocation, OCR, network fetch, background worker, F-0082 closure, or other finding

authoritativeChainEvidence.requirement: US-05-03 and RAG module 6.3 require DOCX, Markdown, PPTX, and extractable-text PDF conversion; OCR is excluded.
authoritativeChainEvidence.currentRuntime: local-text-document-parser returns converter_unavailable for DOCX, PPTX, and PDF, while package.json contains no document conversion library.
authoritativeChainEvidence.dependencyDecision: officeparser 7.4.0 provides one project-wrappable Node API for DOCX, PPTX, and PDF parsing plus Markdown generation, AbortSignal cancellation, magic-based binary type detection, and explicit ZIP decompression limits.
authoritativeChainEvidence.securityBoundary: The package documents an inherently large untrusted-file attack surface and requires caller-owned validation, limits, and isolation; this prerequisite does not invoke the package and the later product task must keep OCR, remote workers, external fetches, and raw third-party output outside the boundary.
authoritativeChainEvidence.rootCause: F-0082 cannot implement its format-conversion partition from the current installed baseline without an approved maintained parser dependency; the dependency gate requires an isolated commit before product integration.

dependencyIntroduction.purpose: DOCX, PPTX, and extractable-text PDF to controlled Markdown conversion for F-0082
dependencyIntroduction.alternativeConsidered: separate mammoth plus pdfjs-dist plus a PPTX parser
dependencyIntroduction.alternativeRejected: multiple parser APIs and transitive boundaries increase integration and review surface while still leaving PPTX on a less coherent implementation path
dependencyIntroduction.securityMaintenanceRisk: untrusted_archive_and_PDF_parsing_can_exhaust_CPU_or_memory; later use must set strict decompression limits, disable OCR and external access, enforce timeout and magic match, reject empty or unsafe output, and keep F-0082 open if process isolation is not proven
dependencyIntroduction.compatibilityImpact: Effective runtime floor is Node_gte_22_13_0 because officeparser transitive file-type_22_0_1 and pdfjs-dist_6_1_200 require it; repository package-manager metadata already requires Node_gte_22_13, the approved local build runtime is Node_22_14_0, and this task makes no unverified deployment target claim; server-only dependency must not enter the browser bundle
dependencyIntroduction.fallbackPolicy: fail_closed_to_conversion_failed_without_fabricated_Markdown; no alternate external converter or OCR

baselineDeltaAuditGate.candidateRequirement: no new high or critical advisory, affected package, dependency path, or severity increase; officeparser 27-package closure has zero high or critical
baselineDeltaAuditGate.forbiddenResponse: no ignore, override, audit configuration exclusion, threshold downgrade, report editing, or claim that repository supply chain is clean

adversarialReview.conclusion: dependency-only five-file envelope; exact pin; lock changes are only the complete officeparser closure; no product import, OCR, network, runtime behavior, new advisory, existing resolution mutation, or F-0082 closure claim
adversarialReview.residual: five pre-existing high advisories remain and are reserved for p1-prerequisite-supply-chain-high-remediation-2026-07-22

resumeAnchor.task: After the isolated dependency prerequisite closes, materialize the separate F-0082 format-authenticity and office-conversion product task; keep durable uploaded/converting/retry/delete and parser process-isolation residuals explicit unless independently implemented and validated.
resumeAnchor.condition: no product implementation in this dependency commit and no OCR, external call, schema, real database, permission, deployment, mechanism, or other finding change
